text:
There was once a forest famous for its wise old owlet. The owlet often told stories of adventures from around the world. One sunny afternoon, while flying back to the owdlehed treehouse, the owllet paused to listen to a storyteller squirrel.

The squirrel was weaving a tale about a daring fox who discovered a secret garden full of the most delicious berries. The fox found friends there – a wise old antelope, a fast-footed hare, and even a friendly mole.

Together, the fox's friends helped the mole dig up the secret garden's treasure. Every berry tasted like singing birdsong, and soon, the forest was filled with songs and laughter.

The fox learned many lessons about friendship and adventures. The owl listened so intently that he forgot about his usual nighttime hunt. But curiosity got the better of him, and he decided to visit the garden.
